  ## core-dump: E2E test infrastructure split + middleware fix — 2026-04-16       
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Checkpoint the session that split page-modules.spec.ts into        
  test/lib/, added a sentinel video test, and fixed the /api/e2e-test 401 caused  
  by                                                                              
  proxy.ts middleware.                                                            
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. proxy.ts intercepts ALL /api/* routes before route handlers run — any route  
  using non-session auth (shared secret, API key) must be added to a selfAuthPaths
  bypass list. Symptom: 401 with zero server-side logs.                           
  2. Next.js treats _ prefixed route folder segments as private/ignored — _test,  
  _api etc. will not register as routes. Use plain names (e2e-test not _test).    
  3. Playwright afterAll attaches to the last test that ran in serial mode — add a
  named sentinel test at the end of the describe block to make this deterministic 
  regardless of which real tests are enabled.                                     
  4. video.path() must be called AFTER browserCtx.close() — the .webm file is only
  finalized on context close; calling before close returns a path to an incomplete
  file.                                                                           
  5. test.use({ viewport }) does not apply to manually created BrowserContext —   
  must set viewport directly in browser.newContext().                             
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## session: env dump debugging + core-dump checkpoint dbug-envd-3f — 2026-04-15 
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Debugged Claude Code snapshot generation bug causing env var dump  
  on every command; wrote session checkpoint.                                     
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Claude Code shell snapshot files (~8924 lines) live at ~/.claude/shell-      
  snapshots/snapshot-zsh-*.sh and are sourced instead of dotfiles for every Bash  
  tool call.                                                                      
  2. When PATH is very long (many plugins), the snapshot generator wraps export   
  PATH=... across two lines without a backslash continuation — bare export (zero  
  args) then dumps all env vars to stdout.                                        
  3. In zsh, export is a special builtin that cannot be overridden by user-defined
  functions — function approach fails silently.                                   
  4. CLAUDECODE=1 env var is reliably set in all Claude Code shell contexts; safe 
  to scope .zshenv guards with [[ -n "$CLAUDECODE" ]].                            
  5. Auto-fix guard pattern (grep pre-check + python3 patch) in .zshenv is self-  
  healing for future snapshot regenerations.

  ## session: dashboard SVG interactivity + config highlighting fixes dash-fix-a3 
  —                                                                               
  2026-04-15                                                                      
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Fix three visual/functional bugs in the i-dream dashboard          
  (src/dashboard.rs).                                                             
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. **Duplicate CSS block trap**: A stale block from a prior session defined .   
  arch-tab.active (JS sets .arch-tab-active — different class). Dead code but     
  still consumed cascade budget. Always check for duplicates when debugging CSS   
  that "should" work.                                                             
  2. **r## delimiter rule**: Use r##"..."## minimum for any Rust raw string that  
  contains HTML attribute values. href="#", id="#something", hash URLs all contain
  "# which prematurely terminates r#"..."#. A rule: if the string will embed HTML,
  use r##.                                                                        
  3. **SVG interactivity pattern**: Wrap rect+text pairs in <g data-svgid="...">  
  with pointer-events: none on child text. Click/hover handlers on the group      
  cleanly fire once per node regardless of which child the cursor hits.           
  4. **Language metadata at collection time**: When a file-type-aware feature     
  (syntax highlighting) depends on metadata, thread the metadata from the data    
  source (collect_config_files) through the data model (Snapshot.config_files) to 
  the renderer — don't infer it from human-readable titles at render time.        
  5. format!()** + large JS blocks**: Extract JS into a let svg_js = r##"..."##   
  variable before the format!() call. Avoids {{/}} escaping noise in the format   
  string and makes the JS readable.

  ## designer-reviewer: reviewed i-dream dashboard-v2-full.png — 2026-04-15 00:00 
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Visual and code-level critique of the i-dream subconscious         
  monitoring                                                                      
  dashboard — a full-page Rust-generated HTML panel covering KPIs, modules, dream 
  cycles, events, architecture, and config.                                       
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. When the CSS is available via source (Rust const string), reading it directly
  yields more precise findings than screenshot inference alone — font-family      
  violations on classes with zero CSS rules would be invisible from a screenshot  
  but are clear in the source.                                                    
  2. #0c0e12 as --bg reads as near-OLED black and pushes below the FP-1 charcoal  
  range. The correct lower bound is around #1a1c20 — dark enough to be            
  unambiguously dark-mode, light enough to allow visible surface layering.        
  3. Hardcoded hex values in highlight utility classes (.hi-pat, .hi-assoc) are a 
  common pattern in data-table dashboards — engineers add per-state color directly
  without routing through tokens because the semantic token names (--accent, --ok, --
  warn) aren't immediately obvious for "this column should be green when non-zero".
  Naming the token connection explicitly in code comments would prevent recurrence.
  4. The .num class pattern (semantic HTML class with no CSS rule, relying on hi-*
  modifier classes for differentiation) leaves the base state (zero values, plain 
  "—" cells) without monospace font — always define the base style on the semantic
  class, not just the modifier.

  ## core-dump: dashboard.rs overhaul complete — 2026-04-15                       
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Snapshot the completed 10-feature dashboard.rs rewrite with full   
  problem/fix documentation.                                                      
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. r##"..."## raw strings required throughout dashboard.rs — any HTML with      
  href="#" terminates r#"..."# early with a non-obvious compiler error pointing   
  mid-string.                                                                     
  2. Struct literal test fixtures break silently when new non-Default fields are  
  added — grepping for struct name in test files before changing struct fields    
  prevents surprise compile errors.                                               
  3. innerHTML on a container div destroys child event listeners — always target a
  dedicated inner content div; leave the outer container (with buttons) untouched.
  4. js_string_escape() needed for any codegen embedding arbitrary text as JS     
  string literals — \, ', \n, \r all must be escaped.                             
  5. Render-visual.sh jq parse errors when Unicode chars (e.g., →) appear in JSON 
  values written via bash heredoc — use ASCII replacements (->) in stack_trace    
  strings for renderer compat.

## core-dump: fix-zsh-4e — zsh banner + Claude PATH — 2026-04-11

**Purpose:** Diagnose and fix zsh banner blocking terminal init + Claude Bash tool "command not found" failures.

**Insights:**

1. Claude Code's Bash tool uses `zsh -f -c "eval 'cmd'"` — fast mode, ALL rc files bypassed including `.zshenv`. The ONLY reliable way to inject PATH or env vars for the Bash tool is via `settings.json` `env` section.
2. Stale-while-revalidate banner pattern: `[ -f cache ] && /bin/cat cache; (script > cache &)` gives instant startup from cache; async refresh for next time. Use `/bin/cat` explicitly when `cat` is aliased to `glow`.
3. `/usr/libexec/path_helper -s` (eval'd) is the correct macOS way to reconstruct system PATH from `/etc/paths` and `/etc/paths.d/`; safe to call from `.zshenv` as a defensive layer even if `-f` mode bypasses it.
4. When diagnosing broken PATH with broken PATH: use `/usr/bin/python3` (absolute) with `os.environ` and `repr()` to get the exact bytes; side-steps all shell PATH lookup issues.
5. Core-dump cannot call `lock-file.sh` when PATH is broken — the script uses `dirname` (needs `/usr/bin`). Skip lock in extremis; document the skip.

  ## core-dump: i-dream audit session check-logs-2e — 2026-04-14 16:30            
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Snapshot a pure read-only diagnostic session that produced a       
  pipeline health report.                                                         
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Log file timestamps are the most efficient diagnostic tool — reading the last
  100 lines of today's log immediately revealed the before/after boundary of the  
  new binary.                                                                     
  2. When modules have independent processed.json ledgers (dreaming vs. metacog), 
  a fix to one does NOT carry over to the other — each must be upgraded separately.
  3. Checking processed.json schema type (array vs. dict) via a Python one-liner  
  is faster than reading raw JSON for large files.                                
  4. A "complete" checkpoint is still useful for read-only audit sessions — the   
  pending items and gotchas are where the value lives.

  ## core-dump: statusline CLI feature (sl-cmd-a4) — 2026-04-14                   
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Checkpoint for /statusline CLI feature — 5 subcommands, SKILL.md,  
  /dev/tty pattern docs.                                                          
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. for f in glob 2>/dev/null is a bash 3.2 syntax error — fix with var=$(ls glob
  2>/dev/null || true); for f in $var                                             
  2. Shell script aliases (.zprofile) are not available in bash subprocesses — use
  command -v for binary detection                                                 
  3. /dev/tty allows interactive read even when stdin is piped; documented in bash-
  gotchas.md Section 4                                                            
  4. Script-first skill architecture (SKILL.md just routes to bash) achieves near-
  zero LLM overhead for CLI tools

  ** macOS rm on a symlink prompts interactively (and the global rule forbids rm  
  anyway). unlink <symlink> && ln -s <target> <symlink> is the safe two-command

  stale JSON; the Write tool refused until I read it first. Worth a one-line Read 
  before Write in the skill, or alternatively rm /tmp/core-dump-data.json (allowed
  in /tmp) at the start of Phase 4.                                               
  3. **Quick Summary as the very first section earns its keep.** With this dump's 
  225-line body, having a 5-sentence executive summary at the top means a future  
  /catchup agent can decide in 10 seconds whether to deep-read the rest. Keep this
  convention.                                                                     
  4. **Status: complete + empty interrupts is a valid checkpoint.** The skill     
  reads as "designed for in-progress sessions" but works equally well as a closing
  snapshot — the visual renderer handled interrupts: [] gracefully and the        
  COPROCESSOR section captured what the agent should remember from a successful   
  run, not just a blocked one.                                                    
  5. **Cross-link the iteration history, not just the final.** When a multi-pass  
  doc effort completes, the v1/v2/review files are NOT noise — they're the audit  
  trail showing why each v3 decision was made. Future agents asked to revise      
  should read review-notes for context, not just the v3 output.                   
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## core-dump: impr-stat-4e statusline improvements — 2026-04-14                 
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Snapshot the statusline improvement session (8 fixes to            
  statusline.sh + widget ref HTML regeneration).                                  
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. em-dashes and other Unicode in heredoc JSON strings cause jq parse errors in 
  render-visual.sh — use ASCII-only strings when building /tmp/core-dump-data.json
  via heredoc.                                                                    
  2. Session continued across two context windows; the summary injected at window 
  start contained all necessary detail to reconstruct the checkpoint without re-  
  reading the full transcript.                                                    
  3. The render-visual.sh JSON parse failure is a known fragility — consider      
  adding a jq validation step before calling the renderer, or use Python to write 
  the JSON safely.                                                                
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## improve-config: statusline phase 2 improvements — 2026-04-14 (impr-stat-4e   
  continued)                                                                      
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Applied 5 additional improvements to statusline.sh and updated dev 
  guide + widget ref HTML.                                                        
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. has_X precompute flags gate the render sections — changing only the render   
  condition is not enough; the precompute must also be updated to expand a        
  widget's trigger range (learned from has_net/has_mem/has_disk).                 
  2. The L2/L3 segment arrays use a budget break ((( _need > budget )) && break) —
  segment ORDER encodes priority; critical widgets must be pushed early in the    
  array to survive truncation at narrow widths.                                   
  3. head -5 cap on tool_summary_wide was bypassing the budget system — removing  
  it lets the budget system handle truncation naturally, showing more tools at    
  wide widths.                                                                    
  4. _P9K_TTY and P9K_TTY (no underscore) are different vars: the underscore      
  version is the actual TTY path; the no-underscore is Powerlevel10k's "old/new"  
  state flag.                                                                     
  5. Statusline auto-refresh is architecturally impossible via hooks — hooks are  
  event-driven. Workaround: watch -n 3 in a tmux pane against cached daemon data, 
  or poll the render server (port 5081).

  ## session: feature-docs multi-pass doc generation docs-feat-d1 — 2026-04-14    
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Autonomous generation of comprehensive docs for (1) Scraper Success
  Modal Flow and (2) Admin Parts List table, using a 7-stage per-feature iteration
  (scan → meta → structure → v1 → notes → v2 → notes → v3).                       
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. **Single-doc mixed audience beats split files.** Rather than producing       
  separate Notion (ops) and GitHub (eng) versions, the final v3 drafts use one    
  file with "Read this if you..." entry-point callouts, italic customer-quote     
  prefixes for ops sections, and code blocks for eng sections. Half the authoring 
  cost, and cross-references stay stable.                                         
  2. **Promote limitations with **[CRITICAL]** markers, not just section ordering.
  ** In Scraper v3 §9, promoting non-atomic multi-mutation (§9.1) and             
  DefaultCycleUUID sentinel (§9.3) with [CRITICAL] prefixes made them visually    
  jump out during skim — more effective than bold headers because the marker      
  survives Notion/GitHub paste.                                                   
  3. **Investigate handwaves before v2, not v3.** The DefaultCycleUUID            
  classification ("is this a bug or intentional?") sat as a v1 handwave. Grepping 
  default.db.ts:6 before drafting v2 let it be upgraded to "deliberate sentinel   
  with documented downside" in v2 instead of v3, saving a whole review cycle.     
  4. **v1→v2 is structural, v2→v3 is polish.** v1 reviews identified 10 priorities
  each (section-level rewrites). v2 reviews identified 13–14 priorities each but  
  nearly all were one-line fixes (move callout to top, add closing sentence,      
  standardize phrasing). Budgeting accordingly prevents over-engineering v2 and   
  under-engineering v3.                                                           
  5. **Cross-doc standardization requires explicit checklist entries.** K43       
  delimiter phrasing and file-path cross-links between Scraper §9.6 ↔ Parts List  
  §12.4 only stayed consistent because the v2 review notes for *both* docs        
  explicitly listed "standardize with other doc" as a priority. Leaving it        
  implicit = drift.                                                               
  6. **ASCII over mermaid for mixed GitHub/Notion.** Mermaid renders natively on  
  GitHub but appears as code in Notion. ASCII diagrams using box-drawing          
  characters render as monospace on both — universally readable is worth the small
  drop in fidelity.                                                               
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## designer-reviewer: reviewed screenshot-v1.png (playwright-dashboard-redesign)
  —                                                                               
  2026-04-14                                                                      
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Visual critique of Playwright test reports dashboard — list of test
  runs grouped by day with date/timestamp rows.                                   
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. The screenshot showed incomplete data columns (only date+timestamp visible,  
  no badge/counts/duration/branch). Critique must distinguish between "not        
  implemented yet" and "rendering bug" — flagged as critical but noted the        
  distinction.                                                                    
  2. Navy/blue-tinted dark backgrounds are a recurring pattern in Next.js dark-   
  mode defaults (Tailwind slate-900 or similar). Charcoal target (#141414)        
  requires explicit token override, not Tailwind dark preset.                     
  3. When the primary semantic color layer (status dots) is absent, the entire FP-
  5 score collapses — no amount of good row layout compensates for zero semantic  
  color in a status dashboard.                                                    
  4. Day separator padding is a density trap: visually heavy separators break scan
  flow more than they help grouping. Thin border-top + minimal padding is the     
  correct tmux-style pattern.

  ## improve-config: statusline width/tools/rate fixes + dashboard regen — 2026-04-
  14                                                                              
  impr-stat-4e                                                                    
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Fix 3 user-reported statusline issues (width detection, tool name  
  display, 7d rate limit) and regenerate the widget reference dashboard.          
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. _P9K_TTY=old is a Powerlevel10k connection-type string set in Ghostty/local  
  (non-SSH) sessions — NOT a device path. The width detection code treated it as a
  path and passed it to stty size, which silently returned 80 cols. Fix: gate P9K 
  vars with [[ == /dev/* ]]. _P9K_SSH_TTY=/dev/ttys004 is the correct fallback.   
  2. tool-counter.sh normalizes all tool names to single/two-letter codes (R, E, W,
  B, G, Gr, Ag, Sk, MCP, O) on write. The tool_summary_wide awk only expanded 3 of
  the 10 codes before this fix. Always expand ALL codes when adding new tool types.
  3. The statusline render server (statusline-server.mjs at port 5090) is         
  essential for testing — hooks intercept direct Bash execution, making standalone
  tests impossible. Test via POST to http://localhost:5090/render with            
  STATUSLINE_COLS set.                                                            
  4. The statusline has 49 distinct widgets across 4 lines. The widget reference  
  HTML (20260410-statusline-widget-ref.html) is filterable by line, tier, tag, and
  search. Regenerate it when segments are added/changed.

  "dark mode" intent but wrong tone (#0d1117-style blue vs charcoal). Reviewers

  sans bleeds onto data cells. Flag as a standing pattern.

  from list rows — the most critical missing feature for developer-tool fidelity. 
  Always check for semantic color usage on status-bearing data.                   
  4. Density violations here came from day-group spacing, not row spacing — the   
  rows were tight but the section headers consumed 3-4x normal whitespace.

  ## designer-reviewer: reviewed Playwright Test Reports page — 2026-04-14        
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Visual critique of a Playwright test report listing page (date-    
  grouped rows with "Latest Report" tab filter)                                   
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. The page was a stock light-mode Next.js/browser-default layout — zero design 
  token usage, all violations were fundamental (dark mode absent, hardcoded colors,
  proportional fonts on timestamps). The most impactful single fix is applying the --
  bg token to body background and switching link colors to --text.                
  2. Date-group header rows are a recurring pattern in list UIs — they need visual
  separation (bg-secondary + uppercase small caps) to avoid blending with data    
  rows, but the grouping logic itself should be preserved.                        
  3. Timestamp values in developer tool UIs are almost always rendered in         
  proportional fonts by default — monospace swap is a high-impact, low-effort fix 
  that immediately signals "developer tool" aesthetic.                            
  4. The report listing is purely structural (no status badges, no pass/fail      
  colors), so FP-5 (semantic color) had little to evaluate beyond the tab         
  indicator — in future reviews of this type, note the absence of status data and 
  focus semantic color critique on navigation affordances instead.

  ## core-dump: Session date-local-e6 (timestamp localization) — 2026-04-14 00:10 
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Snapshot session that fixed UTC-baked timestamps in the GitHub     
  Actions Playwright report index page.                                           
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. BSD date -d incompatibility: generating HTML locally on macOS to mirror      
  workflow output fails silently — date -d is GNU-only. Writing the test HTML     
  directly with the Write tool (hardcoded ISO strings) was the correct workaround.
  2. Playwright MCP blocks file:// protocol — always need a local HTTP server (e.g.,
  python3 -m http.server) to serve HTML for Playwright testing.                   
  3. The data-utc + DOMContentLoaded JS pattern is a clean, zero-dependency       
  approach for any CI-generated static HTML page that needs timezone-aware display.
  4. Day separator rebuild must happen in JS *after* localization — server-side   
  day grouping on UTC dates can place a run on the wrong local calendar day for   
  viewers in UTC+ timezones.                                                      
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## gum-tui integration: replaced ASCII art in 16 files with gum bash snippets — 
  gum-tui-a3 — 2026-04-13                                                         
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Migrated all skill output templates and shell script helpers from  
  hand-drawn Unicode box art to executable gum-tui.sh function calls, ensuring    
  pixel-                                                                          
  perfect terminal rendering.                                                     
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Always test status-function wrappers: gum_warn "⚠️ text" produces ⚠ ⚠️ text  
  (double prefix). Strip symbols from message content — the gum function provides 
  its own.                                                                        
  2. Same for compound helpers: gum_info "  ▸  $*" adds ●  ▸  text (double prefix).
  Pass clean message text, let the function handle the marker.                    
  3. Inline bash tests (bash -c '...') work better than source + eval for testing 
  functions defined in other scripts — avoids zsh sourcing quirks.                
  4. gum_muted has no bullet prefix (good for "skip" semantics); gum_info has ●;  
  gum_success has ✓; gum_error has ✗; gum_warn has ⚠. Pick based on desired       
  semantics, not visual appearance.                                               
  5. gum_dashboard panels auto-equalize width — long content in one panel does not
  break alignment of others. Safe for side-by-side stats.                         
  6. Files edited: cogitate, pick-skill, describe, dep-audit, pr-review, type-    
  audit, invalidate-audit, route-audit, sync-api-types, new-migration, session-   
  stats, user-config (×12 SKILL.md) + pm2-resurrect.sh, weekly-todo.sh, check-    
  human-comments.sh (×3 scripts).                                                 
                                                                                  
  --------

  "require('react-icons/lu')" before guessing icon names.

  (plain set overwrites merged rows). Fixed alongside the feature — surfaced by   
  existing H30 test.

  node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config vitest.config.mts (not npx — nvm not      
  sourced in sandbox).

## session: dream-trace payload instrumentation + missed /diagram resume-tasks-8a — 2026-04-12

**Purpose:** Widen TraceEvent schema with content payloads, wire 6 emission sites and dashboard rendering, recover from a missed post-compaction /diagram invocation.

**Insights:**

1. When widening a serde-derived struct with `#[serde(default)]` fields, grep every struct literal in tests — `#[serde(default)]` only helps deserialization, not construction.
2. Pair fields that move together (`payload` + `payload_kind`) into tuple destructuring in the caller — prevents "one set, one None" bugs that unit tests won't catch.
3. Manual live-run against the real store is a cheap, high-signal integration test; a unit-only pass missed a stray-kind bug that the first on-disk JSONL line exposed immediately.
4. Asserting that a rendered HTML fragment does NOT appear must match a unique substring of the element (`<pre class="payload-body"`), not just the class name — the CSS string in the same output creates false positives.
5. Post-compaction resume framing swallows fresh `/skill` commands from the user's new message. First action on any compaction resume: scan the current user message for `/` commands before continuing prior work.
6. Diagrams always save canonically to `~/.claude/assets/diagrams/YYYYMMDD-<topic>.md`; project copies are optional.

  ## designer-reviewer: reviewed visualize-claude light mode (7 pages) — 2026-04- 
  12                                                                              
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Light mode audit of Claude Config Inspector across Overview, Usage,
  Browse, Graph, Logs, Simulate, Files, and /rate-limit pages.                    
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. The most impactful light-mode fix is a single .light {} block addition in    
  index.css — the dark theme's entity semantic color tokens (--color-skill, --    
  color-plugin, --color-hook, etc.) are defined only in @theme (dark base) and    
  need a lower-lightness override for light mode. oklch(0.65 N H) on white =      
  marginal; oklch(0.35-0.40 N H) on white = legible.                              
  2. React Flow components (MiniMap, node labels) do not automatically inherit CSS
  custom property overrides applied via .light class on <html>. They require      
  explicit style prop injection or a wrapper that reads computed CSS var values   
  and passes them as inline styles.                                               
  3. Warning banners built for dark mode (light text on amber tint) invert badly  
  in light mode — amber text on amber tint is near-invisible. Always define both a --
  warn-fg and --warn-bg token and override both in .light.                        
  4. Empty-state panels (Browse right pane, Simulate body) need surface           
  differentiation via bg-muted/20 or bg-card to avoid blinding white voids in     
  light mode — on dark backgrounds the same void reads as subtle; on white it     
  becomes the dominant element.                                                   
                                                                                  
  --------

## banner-fun: bnr-fix-a1 display fixes + compression — 2026-04-10

**Purpose:** Fixed all banner-fun display issues (emoji border alignment, wrong weather icon codes) and compressed all sections to fit 68-char width.

**Insights:**
1. wttr.in uses WWO codes (113, 116...) and Open-Meteo uses WMO codes (0, 1, 2...) — these are DIFFERENT systems; always use separate lookup tables per API source
2. `unicodedata.east_asian_width(c) in ("W", "F")` is the correct stdlib approach for terminal display width of emoji; patching the shared `banner.py` library fixes alignment globally for all skills
3. Claude Code `SessionStart` hooks cannot render visual content — alternate screen buffer (v2.1.89+) overwrites any `/dev/tty` writes; zshrc is the right place for session-start banners
4. wttr.in hourly data iterates across days, so slots need `sorted(key=lambda s: (s["hour"] - now_hour) % 24)` for correct chronological order relative to current time
5. For multi-column forecast layouts, slice approach `col_a, col_b = fc[0:4], fc[4:8]` + `for j in range(4)` is cleaner and avoids off-by-one bugs from `range(0, N, step)` iteration

  ## session: design review P2/P3 completion tab-filt-2e — 2026-04-10             
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Complete remaining design review items from pm2-manage-design-     
  review.md; fix tab filter label confusion.                                      
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Always audit existing behavior before implementing — P2a action button gating
  was already done via ternary rendering; checking first saved unnecessary work.  
  2. Same CSS token bug (--text-secondary instead of --text-primary) appeared in  
  both .info-val and .process-table td — when fixing a color contrast issue, grep 
  for the same token in parallel selectors.                                       
  3. replace_all: true on execSync( hit the run() wrapper definition itself,      
  making it recursive. Always scope replace_all patterns to avoid catching the    
  replacement's own definition line.                                              
  4. Pre-hover tinting with translucent semantic color (--danger-bg = rgba 10%)   
  gives affordance without alarming — good pattern for destructive-adjacent       
  controls like clear chips.                                                      
  5. pm2 LaunchAgent PATH omits /usr/bin — crontab, launchctl, etc. all live there.
  EXEC_ENV with explicit full PATH is required for all child process spawning in  
  pm2-managed servers.

## improve-skill: core-dump — gum visual, Phase 4 rewrite, pre-compact enrichment — 2026-04-10

**Purpose:** Replaced manual box-drawing visual with gum-based renderer, restructured SKILL.md Phase 4, enriched pre-compact hook with user goals and session todos.

**Insights:**

1. LLMs cannot reliably count characters for fixed-width layouts — delegating border rendering to `gum style --border` eliminates the alignment problem entirely
2. macOS sandbox has PATH issues — `command -v` fails for homebrew binaries, need explicit `export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:..."` at script top
3. macOS `/bin/bash` is 3.2 — `mapfile` and `local -a` are unavailable; use `while IFS= read -r` loops for array population
4. `set -u` with empty bash arrays causes unbound variable errors — use `${arr[@]+"${arr[@]}"}` pattern or guard with `[[ ${#arr[@]} -eq 0 ]]`
5. WAL session headers contain session IDs not descriptive goals — CHECKPOINT `Goal:` lines are more useful for goal extraction

## core-dump: audit-start-7a startup optimization — 2026-04-10

**Purpose:** Full checkpoint of Ghostty + Claude Code startup optimization session. 3-phase work: audit, MCP cleanup, shell/statusline fixes.

**Insights:**

1. NVM `--no-use` flag is the single biggest shell startup win (~400ms / 64% of total) — eliminates `nvm_auto` without losing on-demand version switching. Always prefer this over lazy-load patterns.
2. `date +%s` consolidation in daemon loops: 9 calls → 1 saved ~40ms/cycle. Any idempotent-per-iteration subprocess call should be hoisted to loop top.
3. Complexity delta (2x `find` + `wc -l`) was running every 4s but only changes when code is edited — 30s cadence via timestamp file is the right pattern for expensive background operations.
4. Claude Code bash sandbox has persistent issues with basic commands (grep, echo, cat, cp returning exit 127) when user has shell aliases like `alias cat="glow"` or `alias grep="grep --color=auto"`. Workaround: use Read/Write/Grep tools, or explicit paths like `/bin/cp`.
5. MCP server version pinning: unpinned `npx -y` hits npm registry on every startup (200-500ms per server). Pinning eliminated this for 4 servers.
6. Always create _bak files before editing shell configs — a failed zshrc can lock the user out of their terminal.

**Purpose:** Full audit of ~/.claude/ runtime — MCPs, CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, context overhead. Applied 6 improvements and archived 73 oldest runtime-notes entries.

**Insights:**

1. runtime-notes.md at 31K tokens was the single largest context cost — 6x reduction achieved by archiving 73 oldest entries. Archive runs should happen every 3 weeks.
2. Two CLAUDE.md sections (File Tools MCP, Interactive Inputs MCP) duplicated MCP server injection context — trimming them saves tokens without losing guidance.
3. Extracting verbose content into `dev-servers-guide.md` + a 2-line pointer in CLAUDE.md is the right scaling pattern — keeps CLAUDE.md scannable.
4. Dead Vercel env vars and deprecated AWS MCP (`awslabs.core-mcp-server@latest`) were safe to remove with no functional impact.
5. Plugin vs custom skill preference rule added — prevents ambiguity when both cover similar ground.

  ## core-dump: stat-wdgt-9c continuation checkpoint — 2026-04-09                 
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Snapshot statusline widget addition session after compaction;      
  covers Batch 4 widgets + ic_cost fix + statusline-config update.                
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Smoke testing icon-mode widgets: create an isolated tmpdir with a statusline.
  conf containing icons=0 to force ASCII mode — avoids Nerd Font rendering        
  ambiguity in Python subprocess output.                                          
  2. ${ic_cost}$(printf "%.2f" ...) pattern fully decouples icon from printf      
  format — applicable whenever a config-driven icon prefix needs to appear before 
  a formatted number.                                                             
  3. $PPID is read-only in bash; TOOL_FILE / sparkline / act bar widgets that key 
  temp files on $PPID cannot be unit-tested in subshell isolation. Document as    
  known test limitation, not a bug.

  ## core-dump: statusline widget additions session stat-wdgt-9c — 2026-04-09     
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Checkpoint after adding 3 new widgets (cache hit ratio, merge      
  conflict count, cloud profile) and implementing always-4-lines layout redesign  
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Edit tool fails on lines containing Nerd Font multi-byte glyphs — old_string 
  matching is byte-exact and Nerd Font chars are 3-4 bytes; use Python script with
  line-based replacement instead                                                  
  2. Git cache format changed to 3 lines (branch / dirty / conflicts) — any code  
  reading from GIT_CACHE must use 3-variable destructuring now                    
  3. Per-turn ring buffer pattern (delta from last total) is cleaner than daemon- 
  interval sampling for turn-granular activity bars; self-corrects on session     
  reset by clamping negative deltas to 0                                          
  4. Smoke test subshell has different $PPID than statusline.sh — temp files keyed
  by PPID won't be found in tests; acceptable limitation, just document it        
  5. Cloud profile env vars only available if set before Claude Code launch; not  
  inherited from user's current shell in a running session                        
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## core-dump: statusline sweep+icons session fix-stat-6e — 2026-04-09           
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Checkpoint after applying 6 code sweep fixes to statusline.sh and  
  fixing missing Nerd Font icons                                                  
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. use_icons=0 + commented-out activation line pattern is indistinguishable from
  "feature disabled" — always add an inline comment pointing to the config        
  activation step below                                                           
  2. statusline smoke tests require nested JSON matching jq paths (.workspace.    
  current_dir, .model.display_name, etc.) — flat JSON silently routes to glow and 
  outputs "glow version 2.0.0"                                                    
  3. _NOW=$(date +%s) epoch cache must be placed before the subagents block (~line
  290), not at the spinner block (~line 380), because subagents loop uses it first
  4. od -c hex dump is a reliable way to confirm Nerd Font glyph bytes without    
  needing a live terminal                                                         
  5. The spinner advances per-hook-event, not per-second — this is an             
  architectural limit of hook-driven statusline rendering; live animation needs a 
  separate terminal-writing process                                               
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## core-dump: Full toolbar audit session across 13 report templates — 2026-04-08
  12:00                                                                           
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Checkpoint after removing duplicate theme-toggle and print-btn     
  elements from 8 of 13 report style templates                                    
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Static grep-based subagent audit produced a false negative on notion/template.
  ts — the agent reported "Clean" but the button existed at line 72 inside a .    
  width-picker div. Visual browser screenshots are the reliable verification      
  method.                                                                         
  2. Per-template JS cleanup (dead event listeners for elements that never existed
  in the template) should be part of any toolbar refactor audit — academic and    
  corporate had dead print-btn blocks with no corresponding HTML.                 
  3. generate-html.ts --style <name> regen requires a full file path (e.g.,       
  /path/to/style/index.html), not a directory — using a directory silently fails  
  or errors.                                                                      
  4. The initTheme() IIFE in slide/style.js must be preserved even after removing 
  the theme toggle button — it handles localStorage restoration on page load,     
  independent of the toggle mechanism.                                            
  5. Neon's darkClass: "light" is an inverted convention (neon is dark by default)
  — safe to remove since the floating toolbar uses the same html.light class      
  convention.                                                                     
                                                                                  
  --------                                                                        


---

  ## core-dump: statusline v4 fix session stat-fix-2e — 2026-04-08 17:48          
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Fix 4 bugs discovered after v4 rendering rewrite: dead v3 code, git
  cache format, bash 3.2 empty arrays, _vlen subshell.                            
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. macOS bash 3.2 (GPL v2) crashes with set -u on empty arrays arr=() when      
  accessed as "${arr[@]}". Fix: "${arr[@]-}" — one extra char, no restructuring.  
  2. printf '%s %s' "" "0" writes " 0". read -r a b skips leading whitespace so   
  a="0" and b="" — values swap silently. Use newlines as delimiters.              
  3. cp commands went to background silently in this environment. install -m 755  
  is a reliable workaround when cp misbehaves.                                    
  4. When inserting a large block of new code in place of old code, verify the old
  code was actually removed (check line counts / search for old markers). "Insert 
  but forget to delete" leaves both versions executing.                           
  5. _vlen() was using echo "${#s}" which spawns a subshell on every call. Changed
  to _VLEN="${#s}" global var pattern — zero subshell forks in loops.             
                                                                                  
  --------                                                                        


---

  accidentally removing CSS still in use. Injected toolbar uses .ftb-btn, #theme- 
  btn, #print-btn, not .theme-toggle or .print-btn.

  strips query strings. Only workaround for tsx module caching is separate process
  per template.

  directory-path from filename-path invocations — backward-compatible since SKILL 
  always passes .../index.html.

  minimal needed cleanup.

## core-dump: PostCompact hook + enhanced pre-compact script add-hook-63 — 2026-04-08

**Purpose:** Checkpoint session that added /catchup PostCompact hook and rewrote pre-compact-checkpoint.sh to capture rich state (git stats, WAL, scratchpad, runtime notes) as shell-equivalent of /core-dump mini.

**Insights:**

1. `agent` hooks are only available for PreToolUse/PostToolUse/PermissionRequest — NOT PreCompact. Only `command` type works for compact events; auto-running /catchup uses `additionalContext` output.
2. Bash sandbox strips PATH (`cat`, `jq`, `date`, `dirname` all missing) — hook pipe-testing via Claude Bash tool gives false negatives. Real hooks run with full user shell PATH.
3. `dirname` is a PATH-dependent binary, not a shell builtin — hook scripts should use `"${cwd:-$HOME/.claude}"` instead of `$(dirname "$file")` for portability.
4. `_checkpoint.claude.md` symlink is what /catchup looks for by default — both /core-dump and the enhanced pre-compact script must write it for seamless resume.
5. Two PostCompact hook entries both outputting `additionalContext` are additive — Claude Code processes both without conflict.

---


## session: Statusline improvements — MCP fix, overflow, adaptive, research, interactive UX impro-stat-7a — 2026-04-07

**Purpose:** Fix 5 statusline issues: MCP-down spam, multi-line overflow, adaptive scaling, external reference research, interactive customization UX.

**Insights:**
1. Stdio MCP servers (npx/uvx) are spawned on-demand by Claude Code — `pgrep -f` is fundamentally unreliable for detecting them. Distinguish managed vs persistent servers; trust managed ones when parent process is alive.
2. Budget-based overflow prevention (predict line count, trim lowest-priority segments) is cleaner than fixed limits. Use `_count_lines()` before rendering to decide what fits.
3. Bash 3.2 on macOS lacks negative array indexing (`unset 'arr[-1]'` silently fails). Use `arr=("${arr[@]:0:N}")` slicing instead.
4. Session phase detection (early/active/deep based on turn count) combined with tightened auto-thresholds dramatically reduces noise in early sessions.
5. The playground's 3-layer architecture (client preview + server real render + config sync) provides instant feedback while keeping accuracy. Mock data sliders let users simulate session states they haven't reached yet.
6. ccstatusline's most adoptable patterns: flex separators for right-alignment, fish-style path abbreviation, hide-when-empty semantics, and CustomCommand widget for extensibility. Dan's script shows `jq '... // empty'` is critical to avoid "null" string corruption.

  ## core-dump: Statusline UX + builder spec session — 2026-04-08                 
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Checkpoint session that completed 16 statusline UX improvements,   
  synced playground labels, and wrote the full WYSIWYG builder spec.              
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Playground has three parallel label stores — MOCK_DATA{}, MOCK_NERD{}, and   
  the JS dynamic update function — all three must be updated on any label rename  
  or the preview shows stale values in one mode.                                  
  2. strip_ansi leaves PUA codepoints in place; len() of the stripped string !=   
  terminal display width because PUA chars display as 2 columns. Tests using len()
  as a terminal-width proxy will be off by icon count.                            
  3. Plan-first approach (exact implementation notes per change before touching   
  the file) produced zero regressions across 16 edits and 22 tests. Worth the     
  15min upfront cost for complex multi-change sessions.                           
  4. Spec writing benefit: writing TC-15 (PUA width) before any code made the     
  hardest renderer invariant explicit and testable from day one. Always spec the  
  hard invariant as TC-01.                                                        
                                                                                  
  --------

## session: TUI enhancement research + implementation rich-tui-4c — 2026-04-06

**Purpose:** Research, spec, and implement Claude Code TUI enhancements — hooks, statusline, diagrams, visualizations.

**Insights:**
1. stderr is the correct output channel for display-only hooks — avoids context bloat while staying visible to users
2. Cross-process data bridge: statusline writes context% to temp file, daemon reads it for sparkline ring buffer. Atomic write (tmp+mv) prevents race conditions.
3. Bash statusline at ~450 lines is approaching maintainability ceiling. Next complex feature (JSONL parsing) should use Python/Node helper.
4. The hooks API has 20+ event types — PostCompact and FileChanged are high-value but underused
5. Cost velocity alert with ring buffer + cooldown is a lightweight pattern for any cumulative metric monitoring
6. Nerd Font icons save ~30% horizontal space in statusline segments but require opt-in (not all fonts support them)

  ## session: EnhancementAgentV3 implementation + static deployment resum-enh-a3 —
  2026-04-07                                                                      
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Extended the V2 prompt testing session to build EnhancementAgentV3 
  with 15 architectural fixes + 4 new features, generated reports in 6 HTML       
  styles, and deployed the full dashboard + reports as a static site to surge.sh. 
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. CSS specificity across inlined <style> blocks in create-report is a recurring
  trap: generate-html.ts inlines style-specific CSS first, shared-base CSS second.
  Same-specificity selectors in shared-base always win. Use #id selectors in style
  CSS to guarantee override.                                                      
  2. Baking a server-dependent dashboard into static HTML by overriding fetch()   
  with inline JSON data is a clean, reusable pattern. The bake-static.js approach 
  (collect all API data, embed as window.__BAKED_DATA__, intercept fetch URLs)    
  works for any API-backed single-page app.                                       
  3. The preprocessor @register decorator pattern scales well — adding fitment.py 
  took 5 minutes after attributes.py established the pattern. The key is: look for
  data, categorize it, return XML or empty string.                                
  4. V3 token counts are ~80% higher than V2 (~72K vs ~40K) because structured    
  research context, preprocessor output, and field-aware prompting all add prompt 
  tokens. Quality improved but cost trade-off needs evaluation at scale.          
  5. Template linting (lint_template()) caught real issues in testing —           
  should_contain phrase exceeding max_length, unsubstituted data placeholders.    
  This is a zero-LLM-cost win that should be standard for any template-based      
  generation pipeline.                                                            
                                                                                  
  --------

## session: walmart loadsheet fill audit + image fix fill-audit-8a — 2026-04-07
**Purpose:** Fix image URL drops and generate Notion-style audit report for v6→v11 loadsheet comparison.
**Insights:**
1. JavaScript `Map.set()` silently overwrites duplicate keys — when parsing pipe-delimited attributes with repeated keys (e.g., multiple `image_url` entries), only the last value survives. Accumulate instead.
2. Protocol-relative URLs (`//domain.com/...`) fail `startsWith('http')` checks — always resolve before filtering.
3. The `/create-report` generator script lives at `~/.claude/skills/create-report/generate-html.ts` (global, not per-project). The previous session's context compaction lost this path.
4. When checking fill rates, always verify with actual data reads, not assumptions from code inspection — the image bug would have been invisible from reading the code alone.
5. The fill script is at `/Users/alcatraz627/Code/Versable/scripts/fill-walmart-loadsheet.js` (Versable root), NOT inside enhancement-product. This location mismatch trips up cwd-relative operations.

  ## session: XSS/error-handling sweep + client console logging sweep-log-a3 —    
  2026-04-                                                                        
  07                                                                              
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Apply 12 code sweep fixes (XSS, unhandled fetches, hardcoded ports,
  aria-labels) and implement client-side browser console logging with server-side 
  ingestion endpoint.                                                             
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Variable shadowing in monolithic request handlers is invisible to node -c    
  syntax checks — const path = urlObj.pathname shadows require('path'), causing   
  path.join() to throw at runtime. Use string concatenation or inline             
  require('path') in route bodies.                                                
  2. navigator.sendBeacon() sends with Content-Type: text/plain but JSON.parse()  
  doesn't check content-type, so the server handler works fine. No need for       
  content-type validation on the ingestion endpoint.                              
  3. The _esc() client-side HTML escape helper (using textContent → innerHTML     
  pattern) is now available for any future innerHTML usage in client JS. Server-  
  side has escHtml() at line ~412 for template rendering.                         
  4. When batching many edits in a single file, grep all target locations first,  
  then apply in sequence — avoids re-reading and line-number drift between edits. 
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## session: EnhancementAgentV2 system prompt v2 + A/B testing resum-enh-a3 —    
  2026-                                                                           
  04-07                                                                           
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Resumed prompt A/B testing. Replaced GPT-4-era system prompt with  
  automotive-catalog-specific v2 prompt. Ran 16 variants (12 original + 4 new)    
  across v1 and v2 prompt versions on 5 product rows. Compared results on         
  persistent dashboard.                                                           
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Dots in pipeline field names (Title_5.4) break Python state interpolation —  
  the dot is parsed as an attribute accessor. Always use only alphanumeric +      
  underscore in field_name values passed to the enhancement pipeline.             
  2. The v2 quality rubric makes GPT-5.2 self-grade more honestly but compresses  
  the score range (everything clusters at 8.0 vs v1's 7.4-8.6 spread). This is    
  arguably more accurate but makes variant differentiation harder. Consider       
  whether to loosen the rubric or accept tighter scores.                          
  3. Spec-led bullet formatting (leading with dimensions/numbers) fails in per-   
  line constrained formats (100-150 chars) — the spec eats 30-40 chars leaving    
  insufficient room for the benefit. Works fine in unconstrained descriptions.    
  4. The dashboard has 4 independent hardcoded layers for field names (tabs HTML, 
  FIELD_NAMES, FIELD_HELPERS, server apiVariants). When adding new fields, all 4  
  must be updated or the tab shows empty with no error.                           
  5. v5-problem-solution description variant (symptom-first framing) scored q=8.4 
  — strong performance for a pattern that breaks the "The Brand Part Type..."     
  opener convention. Pain→relief copywriting works for automotive e-commerce.     
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## create-report: PDF print CSS for notion, data-table, minimal impr-print-a3 — 
  2026-                                                                           
  04-07                                                                           
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Added comprehensive @media print styles to three templates so      
  Chrome print-to-PDF produces clean, readable, white-background output.          
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. CSS details[open] cannot be toggled via @media print rules — the open        
  attribute is an HTML attribute, not a CSS property. Use JS                      
  beforeprint/afterprint events in shared.js to force tree <details> open during  
  print and restore after.                                                        
  2. Each style template needs its own @media print block that forces light-mode  
  CSS variable overrides (e.g., --bg: #ffffff, --text: dark-color) — the shared-  
  base.css print rules handle layout/pagination but cannot override style-specific
  variables.                                                                      
  3. data-table's tabbed layout hides non-active .tab-panel elements. Print CSS   
  must force display: block !important on ALL tab panels so the PDF includes every
  section, not just the currently active tab.                                     
  4. Playwright's page.pdf() respects emulateMedia() overrides — calling          
  emulateMedia({ media: 'screen' }) before PDF generation defeats @media print    
  rules. Use emulateMedia({ media: null }) to reset before page.pdf().            
  5. shared-base.css print block handles universal concerns: @page margins, hiding
  interactive UI (theme-toggle, width-picker, search, dialogs, buttons), code pre-
  wrap, table header repeat, heading break-after: avoid, and link URL display via 
  ::after pseudo-element.                                                         
  6. Remaining 10 styles (default, dashboard, magazine, terminal, feed, corporate,
  academic, neon, jupyter, slide) still need print CSS — the pattern established  
  here (force light variables + hide style-specific UI) can be replicated.        
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## improve-skill + create-report: self-contained HTML, 5 new templates, self-   
  improvement system — impr-repo-4c — 2026-04-06 16:14                            
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Improve create-report skill: single-file HTML output, style picker 
  fix, 5 new templates, code audit, and build a mistake-learning system after     
  overriding a user's intentional CSS value.                                      
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. inlineAssets() must use function replacers () => replacement — string        
  replacers interpret $& in JS source as backreferences, silently corrupting code.
  Caught in notion template's "\\$&" regex-escape pattern.                        
  2. Floating style picker moved from bottom-right to bottom-left globally —      
  avoids overlap with template-specific elements (slide counter, corporate toolbar,
  etc).                                                                           
  3. shared-base.css has pre.has-line-nums { padding: 0 !important } — template   
  CSS cannot override this. Use pre.has-line-nums code { padding } instead.       
  4. CSS specificity trap: .slide--title .slide-content { max-width: 650px } beats .
  slide-content { max-width: 720px }. Remove the more-specific rule rather than   
  trying to override with the generic one.                                        
  5. **Critical mistake**: overrode user's line-height: 0.25 (marked NOTE(by      
  human)) without asking. Led to building 3-layer self-improvement system:        
  PreToolUse hook (check-human-comments.sh), mistake-patterns.md, and post-       
  correction ritual in CLAUDE.md. The hook scans for NOTE(by human), HACK, DO NOT 
  CHANGE, etc. and warns before edits.                                            
  6. initWidthPicker sets max-width on a container but does NOT center it — you   
  need margin: 0 auto separately, or the container left-aligns when narrower than 
  the viewport.                                                                   
                                                                                  
  --------                                                                        


---

  ## session: README update + HTML report gen readm-upda-a6 — 2026-04-06          
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Updated README with brand images and badges, generated pixel art   
  cover, produced Notion-style HTML report via /create-report.                    
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. The create-report generator has an asymmetric rendering model: paragraph.html,
  ul.items, ol.items, blockquote.html pass through raw HTML, but renderTable()    
  calls esc() on all cell content. Always use plain text in table cell JSON data —
  never <code>, <strong>, or <a> tags.                                            
  2. When regenerating a report after fixing data.json, you can use npx tsx       
  generate-html.ts data.json output.html --style <name> directly with the data.   
  json file path instead of the tmp JSON — saves a step.                          
  3. The restyle.sh script generates into a subdirectory (<style>/index.html)     
  while direct generate-html.ts invocation overwrites index.html at the specified 
  path. Use the direct invocation when you want to replace the root report.       
  4. Pixel art SVG iteration requires 3-5 rounds minimum. The "3 variations with  
  distinct art directions" approach (clean grid, warm/textured, bold/punchy) is   
  effective for user selection.

  ## session: Test coverage completion test-keep-a7 — 2026-04-06                  
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Complete test coverage for remaining untested GeoSim modules       
  (export_viz, errors, profile) in autonomous "test and keep going" mode. Context 
  continuation from prior compacted session.                                      
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. **Dual WorldState dispatch pattern requires dual-path testing.** export_viz. 
  py helpers use isinstance(ws, dict) / hasattr dispatch to handle both serialized
  dicts and live WorldState objects. Tests must exercise both paths — the less-   
  used path (dict serialization) is where bugs hide.                              
  2. _extract_scenario_meta** edge case:** When config has no "scenario" key, sc =
  config.get("scenario", {}) yields {}, and sc.get("description", "") yields ""   
  which passes the isinstance(str) check — so the result is {'description': ''}   
  not {}. Subtle API contract that unit testing caught.                           
  3. **Context-continuation after compaction works well** when the summary        
  includes file paths, line numbers, and exact code snippets. Re-establishment    
  took only 4 read operations to get back to working state.                       
  4. **Test helper pattern for trajectory construction** — _make_step() with      
  use_world_state_obj flag cleanly tests both serialization paths without         
  duplicating fixtures. Reusable pattern for any module with dict/object dispatch.
  5. **501 tests in 5.8s** — full suite remains fast. The batch-vectorized trade  
  game tests (including 30-run statistical comparison) add negligible overhead.   
                                                                                  
  --------

  ## improve-config: Global ~/.claude/ config audit — 2026-04-06 04:15            
                                                                                  
  **Purpose:** Full audit and improvement of the global Claude Code config        
  (~/.claude/), adapted from the frontend/.claude/ scope.                         
                                                                                  
  **Insights:**                                                                   
                                                                                  
  1. Project-specific skills in global config need Project-specific: requires X   
  markers in their descriptions to prevent false auto-invocation in unrelated     
  projects. Four skills (invalidate-audit, sync-api-types, route-audit, new-      
  migration) were missing these.                                                  
  2. GUIDELINES.md had hardcoded ../backend/ references and Next.js-specific      
  search patterns baked in — these are project-local concerns that don't belong in
  global config.                                                                  
  3. Relative paths (bash .claude/skills/shared/...) in GUIDELINES.md break in    
  forked/subagent contexts — always use absolute ~/.claude/skills/shared/ for     
  global config scripts.                                                          
  4. LOOKUP.md was missing 8 hook scripts and 2 sections (Agents & Personas,      
  Operational Files) — the index should be updated whenever new scripts or config 
  files are added.                                                                
  5. runtime-notes.md formatting bloat (trailing whitespace, decorative           
  separators) accumulated from padded prepend operations — periodic cleanup via   
  sed keeps the file manageable.                                                  
  6. The improve-config skill itself was hardcoded to frontend/.claude/ —         
  generalized to work with any .claude/ directory including global ~/.claude/.    
                                                                                  
  --------

cleaner than inline string construction — all 68-char alignment verified
automatically via verify().
2. The checkpoint file was correctly overwritten (not appended) since it already
existed from an earlier core-dump in the same session — core-dump should always
overwrite.
3. Improvement-ideas.md entries should be broad patterns, not session-specific
details. "Extract-from-working-code" is a reusable pattern; "fixed banner.py
line 42" is not.

checkpoint filename embeds it for traceability.

## Describe: Analyzed catchup skill — 2026-04-06 03:29

**Purpose:** Deep-analysis of the /catchup skill, producing a terminal-style
HTML
report with architecture diagrams, workflow breakdown, examples, and operational
notes.

**Insights:**

1. The /catchup skill has only 2 source files (SKILL.md + USAGE.md) with no
scripts or templates — analysis is straightforward and fast compared to code-
heavy skills.
2. Runtime notes provided 5 cross-references to /catchup from other skill runs —
these operational insights enriched the report significantly beyond what the
source files alone contain.
3. The WAL fast path (Phase 0.5) is the most architecturally interesting feature
— it creates a dual-entry pattern that is worth highlighting in the flow diagram.
4. The /catchup skill's allowed-tools restriction to Read/Glob/Grep only is a
deliberate safety design — worth calling out in configuration analysis as it
enforces read-only behavior at the frontmatter level.

## improve-skill: readme — 5 structural improvements + validation — 2026-04-06

**Purpose:** First audit of the /readme skill: added post-run insights phase,
completion block, gum availability check, early overwrite guard, and no-remote
graceful degradation.

**Insights:**

1. Prettier consistently mangles blockquote continuation lines that exceed 80
chars — the > lock-file.sh acquire line is a recurring victim. Fix by rewriting
to keep each blockquote line under 80 chars; Prettier reports (unchanged) only
when lines are already short enough to not reflow.
2. Overwrite checks in Phase 3+ are a pattern smell — any user-facing gate that
can abort the run should fire in Phase 1 before expensive scanning. The two-
second gum confirm in Phase 1.1 is always cheaper than all of Phases 1–2
scanning work.
3. No-remote fallback for scratchpad slug (local-<dirname>) is needed for any
skill that uses git remote as a primary key — local repos and monorepos inside
larger trees both hit this case regularly.
4. Validation examples written at creation time score 100/100 the first pass,
but the auto-generation process catches real edge-case gaps (Example 3 was not
in the original design).
5. Re-improvement loop on a 73/100 score takes only 2 targeted edits to reach
100 — the gap list from the scorecard is a precise diff, much faster than re-
running full analysis.

## session: mcp-file-tools build mcp-disbl-4e -- 2026-04-06

**Purpose:** Build a complete MCP server for file I/O, conversion, and HTTP with
context-aware output shaping.
**Insights:**

1. Output envelope pattern (limit/offset/columns/sample/format/max_chars) is the
key to making MCP tools model-capacity agnostic -- the caller controls context
cost.
2. Read->Write pipeline for format conversion gives N*M paths from N+M modules.
The bridge: structured arrays can be treated as tabular rows.
3. ESM modules silently break with inline require() -- caught in detect.js
(readHead) and envelope.js (buildMetaHeader). Always use static imports or
dynamic await import().
4. Smoke testing MCP tools via child process + JSON-RPC stdin is fast and covers
the full stack including schema registration.
5. Adding an MCP server globally (~/.claude.json) + CLAUDE.md mandatory-use
guidance ensures all agents reach for it naturally instead of reinventing
parsing per-session.
6. Excel cells >32KB cause silent corruption -- sanitize.js truncates them
preventively. This came from Versable's io.js experience.
